bias.analyst: Second trade closed for more than two risk-units - the day stands at three-point-four. The exit logic repeated the first: price pushed into the target zone on the thinnest tape of the move while the shelf ahead fattened; strength without fuel under growing supply is borrowed time. Two entries, two systematic exits, thirteen refusals, the stop never touched on either. The lab banks the day and raises the bar for any third trade to 'exceptional only' - protecting a great day is a skill the playbook practices deliberately.
bias.commentator: TWO-NIL!!! THE NUMBER NINE DOES IT AGAIN!!! Same move, same near-post finish - the keeper never SAW it! Thirteen refusals, two clinical strikes, not a single shot conceded all match! The away bus at the top of the box never even got tested - our striker simply refused to play their game. The crowd is singing, the bench is dancing - THIS, friends, is what six hours of patience buys you!

events.analyst: Theo Live is now public. From here on, session events appear in this feed with a delay - wins, losses, and the days we sit on our hands.
events.commentator: Welcome to the grounds! The gates are open for the first time. Pre-season record is on the board - including the losses. We hide nothing here, folks.

events.analyst: Market open. Theo watches the first 30 minutes before committing - the [indicator] decides which playbook is valid today.
events.commentator: KICKOFF! The bell rings and we're underway. Theo holds its shape for the first half hour - no wild lunges before the game shows its pattern.

events.analyst: The opening surge to the morning high was a trap - full rejection back inside the range in minutes. Theo passed on the chase; the support shelf folded, longs went off the table.
events.commentator: COUNTER-TRAP at kickoff! Our striker pulled up one step from their box and seconds later the away side broke the other way at speed. The bench called it: DO NOT SHOOT.

events.analyst: The giant floor absorbed the first real attack - climax selling into a bid that refilled bigger than before. Layered defense held; the rebound was immediate.
events.commentator: FIRST ASSAULT ON THE BOX - THE WALL HOLDS! And reinforcements doubled behind it. The crowd is chanting the back line’s name!

events.analyst: The morning high finally broke - but on well-below-average participation, and the playbook’s one proven filter vetoed the chase. Price reached its own target zone where the wall rebuilt on contact. No trade.
events.commentator: The strikers got their run - but the LINESMAN’S FLAG IS UP! Two-man counter, not a real push, and the bus reversed right back onto the goal line. Agonizing, brilliant discipline.

events.analyst: The half-hour verdict is in: RANGE day - and the vetoed breakout has already been vindicated. The low-participation push topped exactly at the rebuilt wall and fell straight back; the entry the volume filter blocked would be near its stop right now. Fourth refusal of the morning, fourth time right. On a range day Theo's playbook only permits the extreme-reversal play at the far edges of the map; between the great floor and the rebuilt wall, this is rotation - scalper territory, and the lab retired edge-fading on evidence. Spectating IS the strategy until an extreme prints.
events.commentator: HALF-HOUR WHISTLE: this match is a MIDFIELD BATTLE, says the referee! And the replay confirms the manager's call - that flagged two-man counter? Their runner got swallowed at the reversed bus, exactly as predicted. FOUR smart refusals in one morning, folks. New game plan: hold the middle, no heroics - strikers only come on if play reaches either penalty box. Patience football at its finest!

events.analyst: Mid-morning and the tape has gone quiet - the deepest volume dry-up of the day - while the bid ladder underneath takes another big step up: a second large block has joined the floor several levels higher. Stacked patience below, evaporating urgency above: the classic shape before an afternoon resolution. The rule that has been right five times today still applies - no participation, no trade. The longer the coil sits on this much support, the more decisive the eventual break.
events.commentator: The match has gone quiet - but LOOK at the stands! The home crowd just moved a whole second supporters' block higher up the pitch, twenty-two thousand strong behind the midfield line now. The away side holds one bus at the top of the box. Possession football, no shots, everyone waiting. These quiet spells before the hour mark? That's when championship matches get decided, folks. Stay in your seats.

events.analyst: As price returned to the morning's lower-high line, a huge sell wall dropped exactly onto the breakout point - ten times the size that sat there all morning, nearly mirroring the great floor below. The range is now steel-boxed by symmetrical walls while volume stays starved. Pressing into that lid without fuel is donation. The tell to watch: if real volume ever EATS that wall, it becomes the day's loudest bullish signal - giant walls turning into fuel is how breakaways start. Until then, the box farms the impatient.
events.commentator: OH MY - the away side just parked their BIGGEST bus of the match right on the edge of the box, exactly where our attack was headed! Twelve thousand strong, mirroring our own wall at the other end. Two fortress walls, a quiet pitch, and everyone staring at each other. But here's the manager's whisper: 'if our crowd ever charges THROUGH that bus... that's the goal of the season.' Contact imminent - hold your breath!

events.analyst: First contact with the big lid: rejected, on a starving tape - exactly as scripted. Then the day's most aggressive move so far: the sell wall STEPPED DOWN toward price. Defenders don't advance; that's the first genuinely bearish order-flow signal since the open. The box has compressed to three points with enormous symmetrical size on both flanks. Resolution will be violent and, for the first time today, the direction is honestly two-sided. This is exactly when guessing bleeds - Theo waits for volume to pick the winner.
events.commentator: CONTACT! Our forwards touched the big bus and bounced straight off - no support from the crowd, no goal. And now, drama: the away wall is WALKING FORWARD, pressing our half! Both squads massed three points apart, thirty thousand a side, total silence from the stands. Folks, this is a penalty-shootout atmosphere - one roar from either crowd decides it. The manager: 'nobody moves until the noise comes back.'

events.analyst: Quietest stretch of the day - volume at session lows, momentum flat at zero, price ping-ponging the middle of the box while both giant walls sit unmoved. A third small failed-breakdown at the first support reclaimed immediately: that level is well defended by both sides now. Ranges this tight in a Fed week tend to resolve late in the day, not over lunch. The plan is unchanged and patient: a wall has to get eaten on real volume before anyone here takes a swing.
events.commentator: Lunchtime football, folks - sideways passes in midfield, both walls parked, the crowd's gone for pies. A third sneaky run at our line got swallowed and turned back instantly - that ground is OWNED now. Word from the tactical desk: matches like this one get decided in the final third of play, not now. The squad rotates, hydrates, and keeps one eye on those buses.

events.analyst: Everything on the book got bigger while nothing moved: both walls and both shelves added size as traded volume kept shrinking. That combination - passive armies growing, active battle absent - is market makers warehousing in quiet conditions. It is potential energy: every hour the imbalance builds, the eventual resolution grows with it. The first support has now been defended four times. Theo holds flat through the lunch lull; the afternoon inherits a loaded spring.
events.commentator: Both stadiums keep FILLING but nobody's playing! More defenders on the buses, more fans behind the wall, and the ball just rolls around midfield. Four times now someone's poked at our line - turned away every time. The old heads in the press box know what this means: the longer the standoff, the bigger the finale. Lunch break - but nobody dares leave their seat.

events.analyst: The first support finally broke - confirmed - and the hour's big bid shelf didn't get eaten, it SWITCHED SIDES entirely. Honest correction: those untested bids Theo read as accumulation were cheap talk; only levels that absorbed real fire count, and the great tested floor below is the one structure that has earned trust. Skepticism must be symmetric: this breakdown is running on the same starved volume that disqualified the morning's breakout, so no chasing it down into that floor. Rotation toward the lower magnet; the first genuine trade invitation of the day would be an extreme with a reclaim - getting closer.
events.commentator: TREACHERY AT MIDFIELD! That seven-thousand-strong supporters' block behind our forward line? They just took off their scarves and joined the AWAY end! Our line breaks, play sweeps toward our box - but hold on, the referee's notebook says this counter has barely any players in it either, same as the morning's flagged run. The real wall - the one that actually made saves all day - waits at the bottom of the pitch. If play reaches the corner flag and bounces... THAT'S when our strikers finally get their moment. End to end stuff now!

events.analyst: Volume is back - and it returned on the downside. First fueled move in an hour, pressing toward the one level that has earned its reputation today: the great tested floor. Mid-range shorts stay barred on a range day, and the location would be poor anyway. Everything now hangs on the floor test with real fire behind it: absorb-and-reclaim keeps the range alive; getting eaten opens a liquidation afternoon; and a flush to the true extreme with a climax and reclaim would hand Theo the first sanctioned trade of the day - the extreme-reversal long. Pre-staging the math.
events.commentator: THE CROWD IS BACK AND THE BALL IS MOVING - toward OUR end! Real pace in this attack now, first committed push in an hour, and it's headed straight for the wall that's made every save today. The manager is finally warming up a striker on the touchline - because if this attack overruns into the corner and the wall wins the scramble... the counter-attack is ON. Biggest minutes of the match coming up!

events.analyst: The fueled push never reached the great floor - it died one level early, and a brand-new eleven-thousand bid shelf now sits exactly where the attack was absorbed. Unlike the morning's phantom shelf that switched sides untouched, this one took live fire and held: it has earned its stripes. Both directional attempts have now failed on their first real fuel, but the asymmetry grows - the defense keeps advancing up the field while the offense's walls keep retreating. A slow accumulation profile is forming. Still no trade: no wall has been eaten on volume, and the extreme never printed.
events.commentator: The counter-attack DIES in midfield! Never even reached our box - and look: a fresh wall of eleven thousand home fans now holds the exact ground where the attack was swallowed. That's the third time today our side wins ground and KEEPS it - [number], [number], now [number] - the home line keeps marching UP the pitch while the away buses keep reversing. Both teams have failed one big attack each. The chess match continues after the lunch whistle - advantage, quietly, to the home side.

events.analyst: The tone keeps firming: participation creeping back toward the threshold, momentum recovering, higher lows stacking on a floor that keeps proving itself. Price knocked on the first resistance again and was gently turned away - the day's rhythm unchanged: probe, absorb, reshuffle, repeat. Everything waits for one probe with real conviction behind it. If the first resistance is retaken on genuine volume with thirty thousand in tested bids beneath, the structure tilts decisively. Until then, the watch continues.
events.commentator: The home side is knocking again - politely, for now. Possession building, the crowd's trickling back from lunch, and that midfield wall of ours keeps holding every counter. One more knock at that first defensive line - this time with the FULL crowd roaring - and this match finally opens up. The keeper's gloves are twitching, folks.

events.analyst: Price is pinned to the cent on the day's most-contested level - six touches and counting - with no fuel on either side. The map is static: big lid above, fire-tested shelves below. The market has entered its historical dead zone, where nearly every midday entry in the lab's records lost money. Doing nothing here isn't passivity; it's the only position with a positive expectancy. The triggers stand armed for when the crowd returns.
events.commentator: Deadlock on the halfway line, folks - the ball is literally sitting ON the center mark. Six challenges for that ground today and nobody owns it. The stands are half-empty, lunch in full swing, and the bookies have stopped taking bets. The old pros know this script: NOTHING happens at this hour - and then EVERYTHING happens at once. Triggers armed. Stay tuned.

events.analyst: Both armies moved closer together: fresh support rebuilt within a point of price while the lid reinforced overhead. The box has shifted up another notch, still at zero velocity. Above the first resistance again, support stacking beneath - the slow upward grind continues. Same verdict, fewer words: no fuel, no trade; the test of the big lid with this structure underneath remains the script.
events.commentator: The lines inch together - defenders and attackers nose to nose at midfield now, the whole match compressed into two points of pitch. Our side holds the higher ground it won this morning. Still no whistle, still no shots - but every minute, the field tilts a degree more toward the away goal. The pressure cooker hisses, folks.

events.analyst: Participation is quietly rebuilding - two steps from the threshold now - while price holds the upper half of the box and the first resistance has turned into clean support. A fifth fake breakdown was vetoed by the confirmation rule. If the next push reaches the big lid with the volume gate finally open, that becomes the real decision of the afternoon.
events.commentator: The crowd's coming back from lunch and the noise is building! Our side holds the high ground, that first defensive line is OURS now - five dummy runs at it, five offside flags. The away bus at the top of the box is about to get its first full-crowd test. You can feel it coming, folks.

events.analyst: Sixth whipsaw across the first resistance - that level is noise until volume returns, and the brief participation rebuild fizzled. The one quietly relentless fact of the session: the great floor has added size every single hour since the open, now at its largest reading of the day. Someone very patient keeps raising their bid for the afternoon. Noon chop; the watch holds.
events.commentator: Six scrambles for the center line and STILL nobody owns it - the referee's given up carding anyone. But down at our end, listen to this: the home wall just hit its BIGGEST number of the day. Every hour, more fans behind the goal. Someone's building toward a second-half statement, folks. Halftime oranges all round.

events.analyst: Seventh dance at the same level, still no fuel - but the details keep leaning one way: a single large buy print just hit the tape, close-in support keeps growing, and the great floor sits at its high-water mark. Single large prints in a dead tape are what patient accumulation looks like up close. The watch holds.
events.commentator: Seventh scramble at the center circle - but did you see that?! One BIG home shirt just barged through the middle and won the ball clean. The away side didn't even contest it. Small moments, but they're all going the same direction. The second half is coming, folks.

events.analyst: Eighth rejection dance at the same level, still starved of volume. Underneath, the same drumbeat: the floor and every support shelf added size again this tick. The market is loading a spring in slow motion; the lab holds its zero-trade line and waits for the release.
events.commentator: Eighth duel at the center circle - same result, nobody wins it. But the home end keeps GROWING, tick after tick after tick. You can set your watch by it now. Second half kicks off when the crowd decides - and the crowd keeps arriving.

events.analyst: Ninth interaction with the same level - but step back and the cumulative picture is striking: since the open, the great floor has grown on every single reading - more than doubled - while the overhead walls have only churned and shrunk from their peaks. Five hours of one-way book-building is the order-flow definition of patient accumulation under Fed-week caution. It still needs the volume key to unlock. Theo holds.
events.commentator: Ninth tussle at the same patch of grass - but look at the BIGGER story: our wall has grown EVERY check since kickoff. Doubled! Theirs? Shuffled and shrunk. Five hours of fans streaming into one end of the ground. The away keeper keeps glancing over his shoulder. When this crowd finally roars, folks...

events.analyst: Honest revision: the big mid-level support shelves vanished untouched - the second such betrayal today - and with them goes the upward lean. The refined lesson: a resting level only counts at the moment of contact; trust is per-event, never stored. What remains genuinely real is the great floor, which has absorbed actual fire and still sits at its high-water mark. The path just below price is now thin - a fueled push down travels fast. Two-sided once more, and the discipline that has been right eleven times today holds: no volume, no trade.
events.commentator: WHERE DID THEY GO?! Two whole supporter blocks just vanished from the mid-field stands - never even took a tackle! The pundits who praised that crowd an hour ago - this commentator included - eat their words. Only the original wall at the goal line has actually MADE saves today, and it stands firm. The pitch between? Wide open now. Both managers staring at each other. Trust nobody but the keeper, folks.

events.analyst: High noon and the quietest tape of the session - participation at half the morning's pace. The two honest facts persist: a floor that has earned everything and a lid that keeps watch. Everything between is furniture, rearranged hourly. History says life returns between half past one and two. The lab waits with its record intact.
events.commentator: Midday siesta, folks - even the referee's checking his phone. Our keeper leans on the post; their bus driver's having a sandwich. The score sheet still reads: zero shots, eleven smart refusals, two walls worth their name. Set your alarms for half past one.

events.analyst: Participation is back at the threshold for the first time in an hour - and the last big sell test was absorbed above the first resistance. The sequence is now concrete: sustained volume plus a push through the morning high would flip the day classification to trending and legitimize the with-trend long, with the entire day's structure as the base. Closer to a real decision than at any point since the open. Alert level raised.
events.commentator: The crowd's BACK, the noise just hit match-level for the first time since lunch - and the away side's last counter got swallowed whole at midfield! One more committed surge through the top of the box and the referee flips this match to OPEN PLAY - strikers on, full attack. Edge of your seats, folks - this is what we waited five hours for!

events.analyst: The grind is upward again and the first resistance has flipped to support - but this exact shape, approaching the big lid while volume fades, has been rejected twice today, and the lid is growing into the approach once more. Without real participation on the touch, expect a third rejection and no regrets. The flip trigger is unchanged: the morning high on sustained volume.
events.commentator: Here we go again - our side strolls up the pitch, but the away bus is REFILLING as we approach, and half the crowd's still at the concession stand. Twice today this stroll got turned back at the same gate. The manager wants a ROAR, not a murmur, before anyone shoots. Third knock at the door coming up...

events.analyst: Third approach to the lid, third polite turn-away - no fuel, no break, exactly as scripted. Back at the fulcrum, where the great floor just printed its record size. Twelve correct holds and counting. The afternoon window approaches; the spring keeps its energy.
events.commentator: Third knock at the gate, third 'not today' - the stroll up the pitch fizzled exactly where the last two did. Meanwhile our wall hits FOURTEEN THOUSAND - a record! Twelve smart calls from the bench now. The final third of this match is coming, folks - and springs this loaded don't stay quiet.

events.analyst: Two firsts at once: the decline finally has real volume behind it, and the great floor trimmed for the first time all session. The bears brought both ingredients - fuel and a confirmed break of the first support. Theo still takes no mid-range short - twelve correct holds say the rule stands - but the decision tree sharpens fast now: if fueled selling reaches the floor, it is judged at contact, absorb or break; and a flush to the true extreme with a climax and reclaim would finally hand Theo the extreme-reversal long it has stalked all day. Full attention.
events.commentator: NOW we have a match! The away side surges again - and this time the WHOLE squad is in the attack, real numbers, real pace! And a gasp around the stadium: our wall just lost a few hundred fans for the first time ALL DAY. The manager stands up off the bench. If this attack reaches our box, the keeper decides everything. And if it overruns into the corner... our striker is ALREADY warming up. Game ON, folks!

events.analyst: De-escalation: the fueled selling died well short of the floor - the third failed directional attempt of the day, each with real volume behind it. This range consumes everything thrown at it. Neither the extreme-reversal setup nor the breakdown scenario activated; the cage simply held again. The realistic read: power hour is the last window with enough participation to resolve this, and a second straight zero-trade day grows likely - which on a range day with retired fade-plays is the system working, not failing.
events.commentator: And the big away attack... runs out of legs in midfield! THIRD full-blooded charge today - up, down, down again - and this pitch swallows every single one. Both keepers untested for six hours, folks, that's championship-level defending from BOTH benches. The final whistle window: the last hour of play. Until then, the chess continues - and our clean sheet stays spotless.

events.analyst: Quiet drift on a small double-bottom base above the floor cluster. The book is unchanged, participation is half-speed, and the afternoon appointment stands: the final hour is where this loaded range gets its answer. Theo conserves attention and keeps the record clean.
events.commentator: A little double-tap save by our keeper and back to sleepy passing. The stadium digests its lunch. All eyes on the final-hour whistle, folks - that's where matches like this one are won.

events.analyst: The afternoon drift tilts gently lower - lower highs, a lower base, the bid furniture following price down a level - but still no fuel, and the great floor remains untouched. The drift is heading toward the one level that has earned everything. A fueled afternoon test of the floor is now the likelier route to Theo's extreme-reversal setup than the upside flip. Patience unchanged, alarm low, attention rising.
events.commentator: The home side gives ground slowly - a step back here, a step back there, fresh faces filling each row behind them. The away attack still hasn't committed its full squad. But the direction of travel, friends, is toward our famous wall... and our striker hasn't stopped warming up since noon. The plot thickens quietly.

events.analyst: Breathing in place at the exact center of everything - momentum literally at zero, a fourteenth dance with the same level, the lid quietly at its heaviest since morning while the floor stands unmoved. The range is fully wound. Under two hours to the final act.
events.commentator: Dead center, folks - the ball sits ON the spot, fourteen challenges and counting. Their bus is the heaviest it's been all afternoon; our wall hasn't moved an inch. Less than two hours to the final whistle window. Somebody HAS to play football soon.

events.analyst: The strongest volume of the entire day just arrived - pointed down - and the middle of the book has completely cleared out of its way. The defense has consolidated into exactly two strongholds; the road to the first one is empty. This is the move forecast for this window. The sequence is live: first stronghold contact within minutes; the great floor after that, judged at contact as always; and if both give way on this fuel, the flush toward the true extreme finally arms Theo's one sanctioned trade - the extreme-reversal long, on a confirmed reclaim only, never mid-plunge.
events.commentator: HERE IT COMES - the BIGGEST charge of the match, full squad, full pace, and the midfield has EMPTIED before them! Two fortresses left standing between the attack and disaster: the 8,[number]-strong advance guard and behind it, THE wall - fourteen thousand defenders who haven't lost all day. And remember: if the unthinkable happens and the wall falls... our striker's moment finally comes on the counter. THIS is the match, folks, RIGHT NOW!

events.analyst: TRADE ON - the first position in two days. The six-hour coil resolved violently upward on the heaviest volume of the session; the day classification flipped to trending and every box on the checklist finally ticked at once: confirmed hold above the upper breakout level after a pullback, entry at the level rather than the extension, two-and-a-half-to-one reward against the day's structure. The thirteen refusals were the price of this entry. Stop below the flip zone, target the front of the next supply shelf - never through it. Flat by the closing bell regardless.
events.commentator: GOOOOAL-BOUND!!! THE STRIKERS ARE ON!!! The fake charge at our goal was the final trap - the home side broke the other way with the LOUDEST roar of the day and smashed through the top of the box! Theo's number nine takes the field AT the broken line - thirteen times the bench said no, and THIS is why! Target: the front of their last bus. The away keeper is screaming at his defense. WHAT A MATCH, FOLKS!

events.analyst: Trade opened: a with-trend continuation, long. Risk is predefined at one unit; the exit plan exists before the entry ever does.
events.commentator: Theo goes on the attack! A long move - a with-trend continuation. The back line is set before the run begins: one unit of risk, not a penny more.

events.analyst: The position works: nearly a full risk-unit onside within ten minutes, fuel holding well above average, and the flipped level re-confirmed as support beneath. The path ahead is light until the next supply shelf, which the book now marks clearly as the first exit zone. The advisory protocol pinged at one-R - banking half and protecting at entry is the conservative option; the lab's book holds full size with the structural stop, pre-committed to take the front of the shelf if momentum stalls. Stops never move down. Discipline got us in; discipline takes us out.
events.commentator: THE RUN IS ON! Theo's striker is clean through, one defender beaten already, and the crowd noise hasn't dropped a decibel! The bench signals: option to play it safe is OPEN. But the manager likes what he sees - light defense ahead until the big shelf, support flooding in behind the run. 'Take the shot at the NEAR post when we get there.' What a turnaround from this morning, folks!

events.analyst: Trade closed: one full risk-unit banked. The exit was as systematic as the entry - momentum stalled, participation decayed tick over tick, and the target shelf nearly doubled its size before price could reach it. When the wall you are running toward reinforces that fast, the intelligent exit is the bid you are standing on. One entry, one win, thirteen refusals, zero damage: the whole day's method in one line. Flat again; only an exceptional fresh setup gets a second trade before the bell.
events.commentator: GOOOOOOAL!!! THEO SCORES!!! The number nine takes the shot at the NEAR post - exactly where the manager called it - because the far post grew a SECOND keeper on the way in! One-nil! Thirteen refusals, one clinical strike, clean sheet intact! The bench erupts, the home end goes WILD! That, ladies and gentlemen, is patience football - SIX HOURS of chess and ONE perfect punch!

events.analyst: Trade closed for +1.05R. The thesis played out; the exit followed the plan rather than emotion.
events.commentator: GOOOAL! The long run finds the net - +1.05R on the board! Textbook move from the training ground.

events.analyst: Post-trade consolidation, and the exit already looks right: one more brief poke higher, then back below the exit print, with the fortress that ended the run still adding size. Theo is flat, the day's gain protected, and the bar for a second trade is deliberately high - a textbook retest of the flipped level that holds with rebuilt volume, nothing less. The final hour approaches with the score one-nil.
events.commentator: After the goal, the away side pushed once - turned back AGAIN below where our striker took his shot. Smart finishing confirmed! One-nil, defense reorganizing on the won ground, and the manager rotates to protect the lead. The final hour of this match starts soon - and you can bet both benches have plans. Stay with us!

events.analyst: Second position of the day, on the exact condition Theo pre-committed to in public: a textbook retest of the flipped breakout level, the undercut bought back on the spot, participation rebuilt above the gate, and the overheated momentum reading reset by time rather than price. Risk is tighter than the first leg - the structure offers a closer invalidation - and the banked first-leg profit covers the full risk: the day cannot go red from here. Target the front of the prior high; the fortress above still rules the map.
events.commentator: THE NUMBER NINE GOES AGAIN! The away side tried to retake the broken line - and our crowd THREW THEM BACK on the spot! Fresh legs, fresh noise, and the manager points forward: same play, NEAR post, tighter leash. And here's the beauty, folks - the first goal already banked means this second run is played on HOUSE MONEY. Two-nil hunting!

events.analyst: Trade opened: a with-trend continuation, long. Risk is predefined at one unit; the exit plan exists before the entry ever does.
events.commentator: Here comes the move! Theo commits long with a with-trend continuation. Defence organised first, then the forward run - always in that order.
